query: status of under construction hydro projects of NHPC

draft.notice: Generated from NHPC's past replies and the selected supporting documents only. Verify every figure and claim against the cited sources before use. This is not an approved reply.
draft.subject: Information for framing the reply of Lok Sabha question regarding status of under construction hydro projects of NHPC
draft.opening: The information in respect of under construction hydro projects of NHPC is as under:-

parts.text: NHPC is implementing the 2000 MW Subansiri Lower HE Project in Arunachal Pradesh / Assam on the Subansiri River. As per NHPC's reply dated 2023-12-11, the project was in the construction stage and likely to be commissioned in May 2025 (2023-nov-jan rajya_sabha s-1443). However, the current status as on 30.06.2026 indicates a present expected date of completion of March 2027, with individual units expected between December 2025 and March 2027 (DOC:projects_progress/9). The physical progress of the Subansiri Lower project was 97.84% as on 30.06.2026 (DOC:projects_progress/9). The project has a sanctioned cost of Rs. 6285.33 crore (Dec'2002 PL) and an anticipated cost of Rs. 30071.97 crore (based on project completion by Dec-2026), with a cost overrun of Rs. 23787 crore (378%) (DOC:projects_progress/9). Reasons for time overrun include transfer of forest land, back hill slope failure, local agitation, COVID-19 impact, additional dyke works, TRC dyke restoration, and miscellaneous hindrances (DOC:projects_progress/9).
NHPC also has other hydropower projects under construction (including JV projects). These include:

- Dibang MPP (2880 MW): Sanctioned in February 2023, with a completion time of 108 months (DOC:projects_progress/9). The present expected date of completion is February 2032 (DOC:projects_progress/9). Physical progress was 17.78% as on 30.06.2026 (DOC:projects_progress/9). The sanctioned cost is Rs. 31876.39 crore (May'21 PL) (DOC:projects_progress/9).

- Teesta-VI (500 MW): Sanctioned in March 2019, with a completion time of 60 months (DOC:projects_progress/9). The date of completion as per sanction was March 2024, but the present expected date of completion is September 2029 (DOC:projects_progress/9). Physical progress was 73.48% as on 30.06.2026 (DOC:projects_progress/9). The sanctioned cost is Rs. 5748.04 crore (July, 2018 PL) and anticipated cost is Rs. 8448.74 crore (Jan, 2024 PL), with a cost overrun of Rs. 2701 crore (47%) (DOC:projects_progress/9). Reasons for time overrun include delay in award of major works, COVID-19 impact, poor geological conditions, flash flood, and increase in scope of work (DOC:projects_progress/9).

- Rangit IV (120 MW): The date of completion as per sanction was May 2024 (DOC:projects_progress/9). The present expected date of completion is November 2026 (DOC:projects_progress/9). Physical progress was 97.34% as on 30.06.2026 (DOC:projects_progress/9). The sanctioned cost is Rs. 938.29 crore (Oct'19 PL) and anticipated cost is Rs. 1888.67 crore (at completion cost), with a cost overrun of Rs. 950 crore (101%) (DOC:projects_progress/9). Reasons for time overrun include delay in award of Balance Civil Package, poor geological conditions, COVID-19 impact, and damage to NH-10 (DOC:projects_progress/9).

- Ratle (850 MW): Sanctioned in March 2021, with a completion time of 38 months (DOC:projects_progress/9). The present expected date of completion is March 2027 (DOC:projects_progress/9). Physical progress was 31.29% as on 30.06.2026 (DOC:projects_progress/9). The sanctioned cost is Rs. 5281.94 crore (Nov'18 PL) and anticipated cost is Rs. 12728 crore (at completion cost), with a cost overrun of Rs. 4616 crore (57%) (DOC:projects_progress/9). Reasons for time overrun include issues with works company, high monsoon flooding, slow dam excavation, skilled manpower constraints, and miscellaneous issues (DOC:projects_progress/9).

- Pakal Dul (1000 MW): Sanctioned in February 2021, with a completion time of 60 months (DOC:projects_progress/9). The date of completion as per sanction was February 2026 (DOC:projects_progress/9). Physical progress was 83.00% as on 30.06.2026 (DOC:projects_progress/9). The sanctioned cost is Rs. 8112.12 crore (Mar'13 PL) and anticipated cost is Rs. 5409 crore (Apr'23 PL), with a cost overrun of Rs. 1121 crore (26%) (DOC:projects_progress/9). Reasons for time overrun include tendering & award of major contract packages, COVID-19 impact, major slope failure, and labour strikes/local constraints (DOC:projects_progress/9).

- Kiru (624 MW): Sanctioned in October 2014, with a completion time of 66 months (DOC:projects_progress/9). The date of completion as per sanction was April 2020, but the present expected date of completion is March 2027 (DOC:projects_progress/9). Physical progress was 87.00% as on 30.06.2026 (DOC:projects_progress/9). The sanctioned cost is Rs. 4287.59 crore (Jul'18 PL) (DOC:projects_progress/9). Reasons for time overrun include delay due to Subordinate Debt sanction issues, COVID-19 impact, flood-related road damages, and delay in supply & commissioning of Tower Crane (DOC:projects_progress/9).

- Kwar (540 MW): Sanctioned in March 2019, with a completion time of 54 months (DOC:projects_progress/9). The date of completion as per sanction was September 2023, but the present expected date of completion is March 2028 (DOC:projects_progress/9). Physical progress was 36.52% as on 30.06.2026 (DOC:projects_progress/9). The sanctioned cost is Rs. 4526.12 crore (Sep'20 PL) (DOC:projects_progress/9). Reasons for time overrun include delay in award of HM and E&M Packages, and strikes by labour union (DOC:projects_progress/9).

- Uri-I, Stage-II (240 MW): Sanctioned in May 2022, with a completion time of 54 months (DOC:projects_progress/9). The date of completion as per sanction is November 2026 (DOC:projects_progress/9). Physical progress was 0.92% as on 30.06.2026 (DOC:projects_progress/9). The sanctioned cost is Rs. 2708.95 crore (at Completion) (DOC:projects_progress/9).

- Dulhasti, Stage-II (260 MW): Investment approval was in February 2026, with an implementation agreement / start date of March 2026 (DOC:projects_progress/9). The completion time is 44 months, and the date of completion as per sanction is November 2029 (DOC:projects_progress/9). Physical progress was 2.99% as on 30.06.2026 (DOC:projects_progress/9). The sanctioned cost is Rs. 2993.96 crore (at Completion) (DOC:projects_progress/9).

Subansiri Lower HE Project was likely to be commissioned in May 2025 as per 2023-12-11 reply.

query: when will the Subansiri Lower project be commissioned

draft.notice: Generated from NHPC's past replies and the selected supporting documents only. Verify every figure and claim against the cited sources before use. This is not an approved reply.
draft.subject: Information for framing the reply of Parliament Question regarding Subansiri Lower Hydroelectric Project
draft.opening: The information in respect of Subansiri Lower Hydroelectric Project is as under:-

parts.text: The Subansiri Lower Hydroelectric Project is scheduled to be fully commissioned by May 2026 (2024-nov rajya_sabha u-617, 2024-jul rajya_sabha u-1233). However, the present expected date of completion for the project is March 2027, with individual units scheduled for commissioning between December 2025 and March 2027 (DOC:projects_progress/9, as on 30.06.2026).

key_points.point: Project scheduled to be fully commissioned by May 2026.
Present expected date of completion is March 2027.

Individual units commissioning schedule: Unit #1 by 20.03.2026, Unit #2 by 23.12.2025, Unit #3 by 01.02.2026, Unit #4 by 08.05.2026, Unit #5 by 15.07.2026, Unit #6 by 15.09.2026, Unit #7 by 28.02.2027, Unit #8 by 31.03.2027.
An Expert Committee was constituted by the Ministry of Power vide order dated 23.11.2023 to assess the ground situation and recommend long-term stability measures for the left bank slope.
The committee has visited the site multiple times, with the latest visits from 20.11.2024 to 22.11.2024.
The report of the Expert Committee is awaited, and its recommendations will be implemented during the current working season.
3 units of 250 MW each (750 MW) are planned to be commissioned by May 2025, and the balance 5 units (1250 MW) by May 2026.

sources.question_text: (c) By when the Subansiri Lower Hydroelectric Project will be completed ; and
sources.answer_text: The Project is scheduled to be fully commissioned by May 2026. However it is pertinent to mention here that an Expert Committee has been constituted by Ministry of Power vide order dated 23.11.2023 comprising of Experts from CEA, CWC, GSI, IIT Roorkee, NIRM, IIRS Dehradun & Wadia Institute of Himalayan Geology for assessing the ground situation and to recommend long term stability measures for the left bank slope. The committee has visited the site from 25.11.2023 to 27.11.2023 and subsequent meetings held on 02.04.2024, 10.04.2024, 18.04.24, 30.04.2024, 23.08.2024 & 17.10.2024. Further, the committee has visited the site from 20.11.2024 to 22.11.2024. The report of Expert committee is awaited. The recommendations of the committee will be implemented during this working season. Accordingly, the 3 units of 250 MW each (i.e. capacity of 750 MW) will be commissioned by May 2025 and balance 5 units (i.e. capacity of 1250 MW) shall be commissioned by May 2026.

sources.question_text: (c) By when the Subansiri Lower Hydroelectric Project will be completed ; and
sources.answer_text: The Project is scheduled to be fully commissioned by May 2026. However it is pertinent to mention here that an Expert Committee has been constituted by Ministry of Power vide order dated 23.11.2023 comprising of Experts from CEA, CWC, GSI, IIT Roorkee, NIRM, IIRS Dehradun & Wadia Institute of Himalayan Geology for assessing the ground situation and to recommend long term stability measures for the left bank slope. The committee has visited the site from 25.11.2023 to 27.11.2023 and subsequent meetings held on 02.04.2024, 10.04.2024, 18.04.24 & 30.04.2024. The report of Expert committee is awaited. The recommendations of the committee will be implemented post monsoon. Accordingly, the 3 units of 250 MW each (i.e. capacity of 750 MW) will be commissioned during FY 2024-25 and balance 5 units (i.e. capacity of 1250 MW) shall be commissioned by May 2026.

query: which NHPC projects are under construction in Jammu and Kashmir

draft.notice: Generated from NHPC's past replies and the selected supporting documents only. Verify every figure and claim against the cited sources before use. This is not an approved reply.
draft.subject: Information for framing the reply of Parliament question regarding NHPC projects under construction in Jammu and Kashmir
draft.opening: The information in respect of NHPC Ltd. is as under:-

parts.text:
NHPC is constructing four Hydroelectric Projects with an aggregate installed capacity of 3014 MW through joint ventures in Jammu and Kashmir (2025-nov-dec rajya_sabha 265, 2025-monsoon lok_sabha 9585). Additionally, NHPC is implementing one Hydro project of 850 MW through Ratle Hydroelectric Power Corporation Ltd. (RHPCL), a joint venture with JKSPDC (2022-feb-mar vidhan_sabha j&k assembly question). 

As on 30.06.2026, the following projects are under construction in Jammu and Kashmir:

1.  **Ratle (850 MW)**: This project has a present expected date of completion of November 2026 (DOC:projects_progress/9). The physical progress was 97.34% and financial progress was 30.97% as per revised/anticipated cost, as on 30.06.2026 (DOC:projects_progress/9). The project has experienced a time overrun of 33 months and a cost overrun of 57% (DOC:projects_progress/9). Reasons for time overrun include delay in award of Balance Civil Package due to security clearance, poor geological conditions, impact of Covid-19, and damage to NH-10 (DOC:projects_progress/9).
2.  **Pakal Dul (1000 MW)**: This project has a present expected date of completion of March 2027 (DOC:projects_progress/9). The physical progress was 83.00% and financial progress was 75.73% as per revised/anticipated cost, as on 30.06.2026 (DOC:projects_progress/9). The project has experienced a time overrun of 83 months and a cost overrun of 26% (DOC:projects_progress/9). Reasons for time overrun include tendering and award of major contract packages, COVID-19 impact, major slope failure, and labour strikes/local constraints (DOC:projects_progress/9).
3.  **Kiru (624 MW)**: This project has a present expected date of completion of March 2027 (DOC:projects_progress/9). The physical progress was 87.00% and financial progress was 71.32% as per revised/anticipated cost, as on 30.06.2026 (DOC:projects_progress/9). The project has experienced a time overrun of 42 months (DOC:projects_progress/9). Reasons for time overrun include delay in Subordinate Debt sanction issues, COVID-19 impact, flood-related road damages, and delay in supply and commissioning of Tower Crane (DOC:projects_progress/9).
4.  **Kwar (540 MW)**: This project has a present expected date of completion of March 2028 (DOC:projects_progress/9). The physical progress was 36.52% and financial progress was 40.67% as per revised/anticipated cost, as on 30.06.2026 (DOC:projects_progress/9). The project has experienced a time overrun of 16 months (DOC:projects_progress/9). Reasons for time overrun include delay in award of HM and E&M Packages and strikes by labour union (DOC:projects_progress/9).
5.  **Uri-I, Stage-II (240 MW)**: This project has a present expected date of completion of November 2029 (DOC:projects_progress/9). The financial progress was 0.92% as per revised/anticipated cost, as on 30.06.2026 (DOC:projects_progress/9).
6.  **Dulhasti, Stage-II (260 MW)**: This project has a present expected date of completion of November 2028 (DOC:projects_progress/9). The financial progress was 2.99% as per revised/anticipated cost, as on 30.06.2026 (DOC:projects_progress/9).

In addition to these, four Hydroelectric Projects aggregating to 3176 MW, including 1856 MW Sawalkot, are under the clearance stage (2025-nov-dec rajya_sabha 265, 2025-monsoon lok_sabha 9585).

sources.question_text: (a) Whether the government has reviewed delays in under construction power projects, including those in Jammu and Kashmir, the project wise details thereof along with the corrective action taken and if not the reason therefor;
sources.answer_text: May be replied by Ministry of Power / CEA. The Projects are being reviewed for their timely completion. The measures includes guidelines such as :  Time bound resolution of disputes and timely payment mechanism should be ensured.  Sufficient power should be delegated at Project level for timely decision of issues at Project level.  Adoption of international Best practises should be ensured.  Availability of all resources, equipment, and manpower, as per schedule.  Equipment should be maintained in good condition and AMC of equipment should be given to OEM.  The new equipment would be purchased by the developer on behalf of contractor, and maybe hypothecated in the name of developer or may also be purchased by the contractor as per the need of the Project.  Settlement of contractor claim should be done in timely manner (ii) Dispute Avoidance Mechanism for avoidance and resolution of contractual disputes of contracts executed by hydro CPSUs at initial stage by engagement of an Independent Engineer has been devised vide MoP OM No. -2020/1/18-15HYDEL-II (MoP) dated 27.09.2021. parts.text: NHPC's financial position for the Financial Year 2024-25 shows a Total Income of ₹ 10,573.41 crore. Sale of Energy was ₹ 8,919.56 crore in FY 2024-25. The Net Worth of NHPC was ₹ 38,348.48 crore and Capital Employed was ₹ 78,229.27 crore in FY 2024-25. The Return on Capital Employed was 6.66% and Return on Net Worth was 8.16% in FY 2024-25 (DOC:financial_reports/8).

As on 30.06.2026, NHPC has several projects under construction. The 850 MW Ratle HE Project, a joint venture with JKSPDC, has achieved 31.29% physical progress as on 30.06.2026 and is presently expected to be completed by November 2026 (DOC:projects_progress/9). NHPC's reply of 2025-12-15 stated 26% physical progress and a scheduled commissioning in November 2028 (2025-nov-dec rajya_sabha 1743). The 1,000 MW Pakal Dul HE Project, also a joint venture with JKSPDC, has achieved 83.00% physical progress as on 30.06.2026 and is presently expected to be completed by March 2027 (DOC:projects_progress/9). NHPC's reply of 2025-12-15 stated 76% physical progress and a scheduled commissioning in December 2026 (2025-nov-dec rajya_sabha 1743).

Other under construction projects include Subansiri Lower (2000 MW) with 97.84% physical progress and an expected completion by March 2027 (DOC:projects_progress/9). Dibang MPP (2880 MW) has 17.78% physical progress and an expected completion by February 2032 (DOC:projects_progress/9). Teesta-VI (500 MW) has 73.48% physical progress and an expected completion by September 2029 (DOC:projects_progress/9). Rangit IV (120 MW) has 97.34% physical progress (DOC:projects_progress/9). Kiru (624 MW) has 87.00% physical progress and an expected completion by March 2027 (DOC:projects_progress/9). Kwar (540 MW) has 36.52% physical progress and an expected completion by March 2028 (DOC:projects_progress/9). Uri-I, Stage-II (240 MW) and Dulhasti, Stage-II (260 MW) are also under construction with expected completion by November 2029 and November 2028/2029 respectively (DOC:projects_progress/9).

query: progress of hydro projects in Jammu and Kashmir
prompt: use the progress report for physical progress figures

draft.notice: Generated from NHPC's past replies and the selected supporting documents only. Verify every figure and claim against the cited sources before use. This is not an approved reply.
draft.subject: Information for framing the reply of Parliament question regarding progress of hydro projects in Jammu and Kashmir
draft.opening: May be replied by Ministry of Power. As far as NHPC is concerned, the information regarding progress of hydro projects in Jammu and Kashmir is as under:-

parts.text: NHPC is constructing four Hydroelectric Projects with an aggregate installed capacity of 3014 MW through joint ventures in the Union Territory of Jammu & Kashmir (2025-nov-dec rajya_sabha 265, 2024-jul rajya_sabha s-2577). The timeline of these projects has been revised due to factors such as the COVID-19 pandemic, geological issues, and local issues (2025-monsoon lok_sabha 9585). The projects under construction include Ratle (850 MW), Pakal Dul (1000 MW), Kiru (624 MW), and Kwar (540 MW) (2024-jul rajya_sabha s-2577).

As on 30.06.2026, the physical progress for these projects is as follows: Ratle (97.34%), Pakal Dul (31.29%), Kiru (83.00%), and Kwar (87.00%) (DOC:projects_progress/9). The present expected date of completion for Ratle is November 2026, for Pakal Dul is March 2027, for Kiru is March 2027, and for Kwar is March 2027 (DOC:projects_progress/9).

In addition to the projects under construction, four Hydroelectric Projects aggregating to 3176 MW, including the 1856 MW Sawalkot project, are under various stages of statutory clearances (2025-nov-dec rajya_sabha 265, 2025-monsoon lok_sabha 9585). Further, 3 Hydroelectric Projects are under clearances and one Hydro project is under Survey & Investigation stage (2024-jul rajya_sabha s-2577).

The projects are being reviewed for their timely completion. Regular review meetings are conducted by the Hon'ble Minister of Power and senior officials at the Ministry of Power, Central Electricity Authority, inter-ministerial committee of IWT and Home Ministry. The projects are also monitored by the PMO (2025-nov-dec rajya_sabha 265, 2025-nov-dec lok_sabha 2672). Monitoring is also done through e-Samiksha, an online mechanism to fast-track issue resolution and ensure coordination between Central Ministries and States/UTs for timely implementation (2025-nov-dec lok_sabha 2672). Measures to reduce time and cost overruns have been notified by the Ministry of Power, including guidelines for time-bound dispute resolution, timely payment mechanisms, delegation of power at the project level, adoption of international best practices, and ensuring availability of resources (2025-nov-dec lok_sabha 2672).

key_points.point: NHPC is constructing four hydropower projects with a combined capacity of 3014 MW in J&K through joint ventures.
The projects under construction are Ratle (850 MW), Pakal Dul (1000 MW), Kiru (624 MW), and Kwar (540 MW).
Physical progress as on 30.06.2026: Ratle (97.34%), Pakal Dul (31.29%), Kiru (83.00%), Kwar (87.00%).
Present expected completion dates: Ratle (Nov'2026), Pakal Dul (Mar'2027), Kiru (Mar'2027), Kwar (Mar'2027).
Four Hydroelectric Projects aggregating to 3176 MW, including 1856 MW Sawalkot, are under clearance stage.
Projects are regularly reviewed by Ministry of Power, CEA, inter-ministerial committee, and PMO.
Delays have been identified due to COVID-19, geological issues, and local issues.

Out of the under construction projects, eight projects are running behind their prescribed schedule (2023-jul-aug rajya_sabha 1132). Reasons for time overrun include initial delays due to statutory clearance, geological surprises, poor performance of contractors, nationwide lockdown due to COVID-19, natural calamities, law & order problems, ban on construction works by NGT, cancellation of tenders, slope failure, frequent intermittent strikes, delay in award of works, and supply chain disruption (2023-jul-aug rajya_sabha 1132).
Specific project statuses as on 30.06.2026 are as follows:

Subansiri Lower (2000 MW): Physical progress is 97.84% as on 30.06.2026. The present expected date of completion is March 2027, with individual units scheduled for commissioning between December 2025 and March 2027. The anticipated cost is ₹ 8448.74 crore (Jan, 2024 PL), with a cost overrun of ₹ 2701 crore (47%) (DOC:projects_progress/9). The original CCEA approved Cost was ₹5748.04 crore (July, 2018 PL) (DOC:projects_progress/9). The project has time over run of 29 months (2023-jul-aug rajya_sabha 1132).

Rangit IV (120 MW): Physical progress is 97.34% as on 30.06.2026. The project has a time overrun of 30 months (79%). The anticipated cost is ₹ 1888.67 crore (at completion cost), with a cost overrun of ₹ 950 crore (101%) (DOC:projects_progress/9). The project has time over run of 3 months (2023-jul-aug rajya_sabha 1132).

Pakal Dul (1000 MW): Physical progress is 83.00% as on 30.06.2026. The present expected date of completion is March 2027. The project has a time overrun of 83 months (126%). The anticipated cost is ₹ 12728 crore (at completion cost), with a cost overrun of ₹ 4616 crore (57%) (DOC:projects_progress/9). The project has time over run of 78 months (2023-jul-aug rajya_sabha 1132).

Kiru (624 MW): Physical progress is 87.00% as on 30.06.2026. The present expected date of completion is March 2027. The project has a time overrun of 42 months (78%). The anticipated cost is ₹ 5409 crore (Apr'23 PL), with a cost overrun of ₹ 1121 crore (26%) (DOC:projects_progress/9). The project has time over run of 30 months (2023-jul-aug rajya_sabha 1132).
Kwar (540 MW): Physical progress is 36.52% as on 30.06.2026. The present expected date of completion is March 2028. The project has a time overrun of 16 months (30%) (DOC:projects_progress/9).

sources.question_text: (b) if so, the reasons therefor and the details of the under construction power projects in various parts of the country, State/UT-wise including Odisha:
sources.answer_text: May be replied by Ministry of Power / CEA. As far as NHPC Ltd. is concerned, it is constructing 15 Power Projects with aggregate installed capacity of 10449 MW. The detail of same is as under: Amount in ₹ crore Out of these construction Projects, eight projects are running behind their prescribed schedule. The reasons of time overrun of these Projects are as given below: *26 MW out of total 65 MW Synchronized and Commissioned on 09.07.2022. 1. PARBATI-II HE PROJECT The original CCEA cost of Parbati-II HE Project was ₹ 3,919.59 crore at Dec'2001 PL. The Project has time over run of 175 months. Now, the anticipated cost of the Project is ₹ 11134.54 crore (July' 22 PL). REASONS FOR TIME OVER RUN  Initial delay due to delay in statutory clearance.  Geological surprises.  Week geological conditions in HRT.  Ban on use of aggregate from August 2007 to March 2008 by Hon'ble High court, Shimla.  Poor performance of contractors and re-award of balance works of HRT and Nallah works.  Nationwide lock down due to covid-19.  Natural calamities. 2. SUBANSIRI LOWER HE PROJECT The original CCEA approved Cost of Subansiri Lower HE Project was ₹6,285.33 crore at Dec'2002 PL. The Project has overall time over run of 165 months. Presently, the anticipated cost of the Project is ₹ 21,247.54 crore (completion cost). REASONS FOR TIME OVER RUN  Delay in transfer of Forest Land.  Law & Order problems (Intermittent Forcible Stoppages / Bandh by locals from Dec 2011)  Ban on construction works by Hon'ble National Green Tribunal (NGT) Kolkata vide order dated 11.12.2015. The construction work resumed on 15.10.2019 after clearance from Hon'ble NGT.  Nation wide lock down due to covid-19.  Natural calamities  Time for executing additional works suggested by Dam Design Review Panel (DDRP). 3. PAKALDUL HE PROJECT Pakaldul HE Project has time over run of 78 months. REASONS FOR TIME OVER RUN  Cancellation of tenders due to less response and higher cost quoted by the bidders.  Slope failure at HRT adit portal.  Frequent intermittent strikes.  Nation wide lock down due to covid-19. 4. KIRU HE PROJECT Kiru HE Project has time over run of 30 months. REASONS FOR TIME OVER RUN  Delay in award of works due to issue related to Subordinate debt for the Project.  Nation wide lock down due to covid-19.  Delay in supply & commissioning of tower crane for dam concreting on account of delayed supply of various parts from different countries. 5. TEESTA VI HE PROJECT Teesta-VI HE Project has time over run of 29 months. REASONS FOR TIME OVER RUN  Delay in award of Major works package.  Week geological conditions in HRT.  Nation wide lock down due to covid-19. 6. RANGIT IV HE PROJECT Rangit-IV HE Project has time over run of 3 months. REASONS FOR TIME OVER RUN  Delay in award of Major works package for want of Security clearance from MHA, GoI in respect of foreign company participating in the tender.  Nation wide lock down due to covid-19. 7. RATLE IV HE PROJECT Ratle HE Project has time over run of 3 months. REASONS FOR TIME OVER RUN  Contractual delay. 8. KALPI SOLAR PROJECT Kalpi solar Project has time over run of 27 months. 26 MW capacity out of 65 MW was commissioned in July 2022. REASONS FOR TIME OVER RUN  Supply chain disruption in PV Module. Imposition of Basic Custom Duty w.e.f. 01.04.2022 resulting into limited availability and sharp rise in the prices of solar modules  Nation wide lock down due to covid-19.

draft.subject: एनएचपीसी की निर्माणाधीन परियोजनाओं की वर्तमान स्थिति के संबंध में लोक सभा प्रश्न का उत्तर
draft.opening: सूचना निम्नानुसार है:-

parts.text: एनएचपीसी लिमिटेड (संयुक्त उद्यम सहित) की निर्माणाधीन परियोजनाओं की वर्तमान स्थिति निम्नानुसार है (30.06.2026 की स्थिति के अनुसार):-

1. **सुबनसिरी लोअर (2000 मेगावाट):** इस परियोजना की स्वीकृत क्षमता 2000 मेगावाट है। परियोजना की भौतिक प्रगति 97.84% है और वित्तीय प्रगति स्वीकृत लागत के अनुसार 424.29% तथा अनुमानित लागत के अनुसार 88.68% है (30.06.2026 की स्थिति के अनुसार)। परियोजना के पूरा होने की वर्तमान अपेक्षित तिथि मार्च 2027 है, जिसमें यूनिट 1: 20.03.26, यूनिट 2: 23.12.25, यूनिट 3: 01.02.26, यूनिट 4: 08.05.26, यूनिट 5: 15.07.26, यूनिट 6: 15.09.26, यूनिट 7: 28.02.27 और यूनिट 8: 31.03.27 शामिल हैं (DOC:projects_progress/9)। परियोजना में 198 महीने का समय अधिरोहण हुआ है, जिसके मुख्य कारण वन भूमि का हस्तांतरण, पावरहाउस में बैक हिल ढलान का विफल होना, स्थानीय आंदोलन, कोविड-19 का प्रभाव, अतिरिक्त डाइक कार्य और विविध बाधाएं हैं (DOC:projects_progress/9)।

2. **दिबांग एमपीपी (2880 मेगावाट):** इस परियोजना की स्वीकृत क्षमता 2880 मेगावाट है। परियोजना की भौतिक प्रगति 17.78% है और वित्तीय प्रगति स्वीकृत लागत के अनुसार 15.39% तथा अनुमानित लागत के अनुसार 15.39% है (30.06.2026 की स्थिति के अनुसार)। परियोजना के पूरा होने की वर्तमान अपेक्षित तिथि फरवरी 2032 है (DOC:projects_progress/9)।

3. **तीस्ता-VI (500 मेगावाट):** इस परियोजना की स्वीकृत क्षमता 500 मेगावाट है। परियोजना की भौतिक प्रगति 73.48% है और वित्तीय प्रगति स्वीकृत लागत के अनुसार 94.88% तथा अनुमानित लागत के अनुसार 64.55% है (30.06.2026 की स्थिति के अनुसार)। परियोजना के पूरा होने की वर्तमान अपेक्षित तिथि सितंबर 2029 है (DOC:projects_progress/9)। परियोजना में 66 महीने का समय अधिरोहण हुआ है, जिसके मुख्य कारण प्रमुख कार्य पैकेज के आवंटन में देरी, कोविड-19 का प्रभाव, खराब भूवैज्ञानिक स्थितियां और अक्टूबर 2023 में तीस्ता बेसिन में अचानक आई बाढ़ हैं (DOC:projects_progress/9)।

4. **रंगित IV (120 मेगावाट):** इस परियोजना की स्वीकृत क्षमता 120 मेगावाट है। परियोजना की वित्तीय प्रगति स्वीकृत लागत के अनुसार 199.27% तथा अनुमानित लागत के अनुसार 99.00% है (30.06.2026 की स्थिति के अनुसार)। परियोजना में 30 महीने का समय अधिरोहण हुआ है, जिसके मुख्य कारण सुरक्षा मंजूरी में देरी, खराब भूवैज्ञानिक स्थितियां, कोविड-19 का प्रभाव और NH-10 को नुकसान हैं (DOC:projects_progress/9)।

5. **रातले (850 मेगावाट):** इस परियोजना की स्वीकृत क्षमता 850 मेगावाट है। परियोजना की भौतिक प्रगति 31.29% है और वित्तीय प्रगति स्वीकृत लागत के अनुसार 30.97% तथा अनुमानित लागत के अनुसार 30.97% है (30.06.2026 की स्थिति के अनुसार)। परियोजना के पूरा होने की वर्तमान अपेक्षित तिथि मार्च 2027 है (DOC:projects_progress/9)। परियोजना में 33 महीने का समय अधिरोहण हुआ है, जिसके मुख्य कारण उप-ठेकेदार इंटरफ़ेस के साथ कार्य कंपनी के पुरस्कार में देरी, उच्च मानसून बाढ़, बांध उत्खनन में धीमी गति और कुशल जनशक्ति की कमी हैं (DOC:projects_progress/9)।

6. **पाकल दुल (1000 मेगावाट):** इस परियोजना की स्वीकृत क्षमता 1000 मेगावाट है। परियोजना की भौतिक प्रगति 83.00% है और वित्तीय प्रगति स्वीकृत लागत के अनुसार 118.82% तथा अनुमानित लागत के अनुसार 75.73% है (30.06.2026 की स्थिति के अनुसार)। परियोजना में 83 महीने का समय अधिरोहण हुआ है, जिसके मुख्य कारण प्रमुख अनुबंध पैकेजों के निविदा और पुरस्कार में देरी, कोविड-19 का प्रभाव, टीबीएम एडिट साइट पर प्रमुख ढलान विफलता और श्रम हड़ताल/स्थानीय बाधाएं हैं (DOC:projects_progress/9)।

7. **किरू (624 मेगावाट):** इस परियोजना की स्वीकृत क्षमता 624 मेगावाट है। परियोजना की भौतिक प्रगति 87.00% है और वित्तीय प्रगति स्वीकृत लागत के अनुसार 89.97% तथा अनुमानित लागत के अनुसार 71.32% है (30.06.2026 की स्थिति के अनुसार)। परियोजना के पूरा होने की वर्तमान अपेक्षित तिथि मार्च 2027 है (DOC:projects_progress/9)। परियोजना में 42 महीने का समय अधिरोहण हुआ है, जिसके मुख्य कारण अधीनस्थ ऋण स्वीकृति में देरी, कोविड-19 का प्रभाव, बाढ़ से संबंधित सड़क क्षति और टावर क्रेन की आपूर्ति में देरी हैं (DOC:projects_progress/9)।

8. **क्वार (540 मेगावाट):** इस परियोजना की स्वीकृत क्षमता 540 मेगावाट है। परियोजना की भौतिक प्रगति 36.52% है और वित्तीय प्रगति स्वीकृत लागत के अनुसार 40.67% तथा अनुमानित लागत के अनुसार 40.67% है (30.06.2026 की स्थिति के अनुसार)। परियोजना के पूरा होने की वर्तमान अपेक्षित तिथि मार्च 2028 है (DOC:projects_progress/9)। परियोजना में 16 महीने का समय अधिरोहण हुआ है, जिसके मुख्य कारण परियोजना के एचएम और ई एंड एम पैकेजों के पुरस्कार में देरी और श्रम संघ द्वारा हड़ताल/बार-बार टूल डाउन हैं (DOC:projects_progress/9)।

9. **उरी-I, स्टेज-II (240 मेगावाट):** इस परियोजना की स्वीकृत क्षमता 240 मेगावाट है। परियोजना की वित्तीय प्रगति स्वीकृत लागत के अनुसार 0.92% तथा अनुमानित लागत के अनुसार 0.92% है (30.06.2026 की स्थिति के अनुसार)। परियोजना के पूरा होने की वर्तमान अपेक्षित तिथि नवंबर 2029 है (DOC:projects_progress/9)।

10. **दुलहस्ती, स्टेज-II (260 मेगावाट):** इस परियोजना की स्वीकृत क्षमता 260 मेगावाट है। परियोजना की वित्तीय प्रगति स्वीकृत लागत के अनुसार 2.99% तथा अनुमानित लागत के अनुसार 2.99% है (30.06.2026 की स्थिति के अनुसार)। परियोजना के पूरा होने की वर्तमान अपेक्षित तिथि नवंबर 2029 है (DOC:projects_progress/9)।

parts.text: As on 30.06.2026, NHPC has 10 hydro projects under construction. parts.text:

parts.text:

draft.opening: The information regarding under construction projects of NHPC with their capacities is as under:-

parts.text: NHPC Ltd. has 8 Hydroelectric Projects with an aggregate installed capacity of 6434 MW under construction stage (2023-jan-apr lok_sabha 110, 2023-dec-jan lok_sabha 11881). As on 30.06.2026, the following projects are under construction:
1. Subansiri Lower Project: 2000 MW (8x250 MW) (DOC:projects_progress/9). The present expected date of completion is March 2027 (DOC:projects_progress/9).
2. Dibang MPP: 2880 MW (12x240 MW) (DOC:projects_progress/9). The present expected date of completion is February 2032 (DOC:projects_progress/9).
3. Teesta-VI: 500 MW (4x125 MW) (DOC:projects_progress/9). The present expected date of completion is September 2029 (DOC:projects_progress/9).
4. Rangit IV: 120 MW (3x40 MW) (DOC:projects_progress/9).

5. Ratle: 850 MW (4x205+1x30 MW) (DOC:projects_progress/9). NHPC is implementing this project through RHPCL, a Joint Venture with JKSPDC (2022-feb-mar vidhan_sabha j&k assembly question). The present expected date of completion is November 2026 (DOC:projects_progress/9).

6. Pakal Dul: 1000 MW (4x250 MW) (DOC:projects_progress/9). NHPC is implementing this project through CVPPPL, a Joint Venture with JKSPDC (2022-feb-mar vidhan_sabha j&k assembly question). The present expected date of completion is March 2027 (DOC:projects_progress/9).

7. Kiru: 624 MW (4x156 MW) (DOC:projects_progress/9). NHPC is implementing this project through CVPPPL, a Joint Venture with JKSPDC (2022-feb-mar vidhan_sabha j&k assembly question). The present expected date of completion is March 2027 (DOC:projects_progress/9).

8. Kwar: 540 MW (4x135 MW) (DOC:projects_progress/9). NHPC is implementing this project through CVPPPL, a Joint Venture with JKSPDC (2022-feb-mar vidhan_sabha j&k assembly question). The present expected date of completion is March 2028 (DOC:projects_progress/9).
9. Uri-I, Stage-II: 240 MW (2x120 MW) (DOC:projects_progress/9). The present expected date of completion is November 2029 (DOC:projects_progress/9).
10. Dulhasti, Stage-II: 260 MW (2x130 MW) (DOC:projects_progress/9). The present expected date of completion is November 2028 (DOC:projects_progress/9).
